title: The Three Little Pigs
content:
Once there were three little pigs.

They left home to build their own houses.

The first pig built a house of straw. It was fast and easy!

The second pig built a house of sticks. It was a bit stronger.

The third pig built a house of bricks. It took a long time.

Then came the big bad wolf!

"Little pig, little pig, let me in!" said the wolf.

"Not by the hair on my chinny chin chin!" said the first pig.

So the wolf huffed and puffed and blew the house down!

The first pig ran to his brother's stick house.

The wolf came to the stick house. He huffed and puffed and blew it down too!

Both pigs ran to the brick house.

The wolf tried to blow down the brick house. He huffed and puffed. But he could not blow it down!

The wolf tried to come down the chimney. But the pigs had a pot of hot water waiting!

The wolf ran away and never came back.

The three little pigs lived happily ever after in the strong brick house.

The End.

author: Traditional

attribution.author: Traditional (English Fairy Tale)
attribution.source: English Fairy Tales, collected by Joseph Jacobs (1890)
